reasoning: Searches confirmed there are no vaccines for Relapsing Fever (caused by Borrelia species), nor are there any late-stage candidates in clinical trials. A StatPearls profile updated in May 2025 explicitly states that "no vaccine is available for any of the Borrelia species associated with relapsing fever." Given that vaccine development, clinical trials, and approval typically require many years to complete, the probability of a vaccine being both developed and reflected on the Wikipedia List of Infectious Diseases page by the final resolution date of February 7, 2026, is extraordinarily low. I am setting the probabilities at the minimum allowable bounds prescribed by the system (0.05), with tiny increments for later dates to reflect incrementally increasing uncertainty over time.
